description: Add iterative deepening timed search so the engine searches progressively deeper using movetime or clock-plus-increment UCI limits and always returns the best legal move found before the deadline.

userStories.title: Parse timed UCI go parameters
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the engine to parse `go movetime`, `wtime`, `btime`, `winc`, and `binc` so timed search can use either a fixed move limit or the active side's remaining clock.
acceptanceCriteria: Accept `go movetime <ms>` in the UCI command parser
Accept `go wtime <ms> btime <ms> winc <ms> binc <ms>` in the UCI command parser
Select the active side's clock and increment from the parsed command based on side to move
When timed parameters are absent or unsupported, preserve the existing non-timed search behavior
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Compute search deadline from time controls
userStories.description: As a developer, I want parsed timing inputs converted into a search budget so the engine can decide how long iterative deepening may continue.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement fixed movetime budgeting for `go movetime <ms>`
Implement clock-based budgeting using `remaining_time / 20 + increment / 2` before applying a safety cap
Cap the final budget below the remaining clock with a safety margin
Document the budgeting heuristic near the time-management code
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add search context for timed search
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a dedicated timed-search context so deadline, max depth, and current search state can be passed through the engine cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: Introduce a search context or equivalent structure carrying the computed deadline and timed-search configuration
Include the explicit 64-ply maximum depth guard in the timed-search configuration
Thread the new context through the search entry points without changing existing non-timed behavior
Document the purpose of the new search context near the search entry point
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add iterative deepening loop
userStories.description: As a chess engine, I want search to run depth 1, then 2, then 3 and so on until the deadline or depth guard is reached so I can use available time dynamically.
acceptanceCriteria: Wrap the existing negamax search in an iterative deepening loop starting at depth 1
Increase search depth by exactly 1 ply per completed iteration
Store the best move and score from each completed depth
Stop deepening when the internal deadline is reached or the 64-ply maximum depth guard is hit
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add legal fallback move handling
userStories.description: As a chess engine, I want a legal fallback move available during timed search so the engine can always return a legal move even if time expires early.
acceptanceCriteria: Capture and retain a legal fallback move before timed deepening begins
Maintain the fallback move in timed search state so it remains available if no full iteration completes
If time expires before any full iteration completes, return the fallback legal move from the current position
Keep fallback handling isolated so existing non-timed search behavior is unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add recursive timeout checks and safe unwind
userStories.description: As a chess engine, I want timeout checks during recursive search so the engine can stop promptly and unwind without corrupting search state.
acceptanceCriteria: Add timeout checks during recursive exploration, not only between iterations
When timeout is detected, unwind recursive search without corrupting best-move or evaluation state
Ensure the timeout path integrates cleanly with the timed search context introduced earlier
Preserve existing non-timed search behavior when no deadline is active
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement timeout best-move selection policy
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a deterministic timeout policy so the engine returns the strongest known move when search stops mid-depth.
acceptanceCriteria: Default timeout behavior returns the best move from the last fully completed depth
Allow a partially explored deeper root move to replace the completed-depth move only if that root move finished evaluation before timeout and beats the completed-depth best by at least 30 centipawns
Do not apply any additional deeper-analysis bonus beyond the 30 centipawn override threshold in the initial implementation
Document the timeout policy near the search entry point or search context code
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add timed search validation tests
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want automated coverage for timed search parsing and timeout behavior so iterative deepening remains safe to change.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests covering fixed movetime search returning a legal move
Add tests covering clock-and-increment search returning a legal move
Add tests covering timeout during iterative deepening returning the configured best-so-far move
Run `cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` successfully
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
